description: To become different from what was before—the universal constant that nothing remains the same. 'Helui' names both the process and the fact of change, applicable to weather shifting, minds opening, seasons turning, and all the ten thousand transformations of existence. It is the verb of impermanence made visible.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'h', the lightest consonant—mere breath, as change often begins imperceptibly. The 'e' perceives the first difference. Then 'lu' flows with liquid transition, the old becoming the new. Finally 'i' rises bright and alert, the changed state emerging into awareness.
grammatical_notes: As verb (intransitive): 'themo helui' (the thing changes). As noun: 'helui nio' (change exists / there is change). Can take directional phrases: 'themo phoi wela thue helui' (the thing changes from good to [something]).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Impermanence (anicca) is the first mark of existence—all conditioned things change. 'Helui' names this fundamental truth without resistance or grasping.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Seasonal wisdom accepts change as natural rhythm—what rises falls, what falls rises, the wheel turns endlessly.
